description: Read-only vault reconciliation auditor for Obsidian. scan -> classify -> plan produces an auditable, human-reviewable report; apply and undo are deferred to a later release.
stance: Effects at the edges: scan, classify, plan are pure. Only apply (not yet built) mutates.
Values, not places: every phase's output is a file.
Decisions as data: the plan is a markdown note the operator reviews in Obsidian.
Policy, not mechanism: bucket -> action is declarative YAML, not code.
safety: scan, classify, plan never mutate the vault. Tests enforce this.
Files matching any known credential pattern are routed to the SECRETS bucket and quarantined. Credential bytes never appear in any artifact.

subcommands.name: scan
subcommands.summary: Walk the vault and emit index.jsonl.

flags.description: Path to Obsidian vault. Falls back to config.vault_path.

subcommands.name: classify
subcommands.summary: Group index entries by basename and assign each pair a bucket.

subcommands.name: plan
subcommands.summary: Consume classification + policy, emit a markdown checklist for human review in Obsidian.

flags.description: Override path for policy.yaml. Missing file is fine — defaults apply.

notes: plan is re-entrant: checkbox state in an existing plan.md is preserved for action IDs that still apply.
Action IDs are a 12-hex SHA-256 prefix of (bucket, type, sorted paths). Stable across runs.

subcommands.name: purge
subcommands.summary: Remove everything under .obconverge/trash/; this makes the affected apply runs permanently non-reversible.

flags.description: Actually remove. Default is dry-run (reports counts).

notes: Default mode is dry-run. Reports file count and total bytes.
After successful purge, undo reports trash_missing for every Applied journal entry — by design.
v1 purges the entire trash tree; selective purge by timestamp or action id is a future enhancement.
Absent trash directory is not an error; purge is idempotent.

subcommands.name: undo
subcommands.summary: Reverse the most recent apply run by restoring files from trash.

flags.description: Actually move files. Default is dry-run.

notes: Default mode is dry-run. --execute is required to actually mutate.
undo iterates journal Applied entries in LIFO order (most recent first).
drop reversal: rename trash_path -> path.
merge-frontmatter reversal: rename secondary_trash -> secondary_path (restore loser); rename trash_path -> path (overwrite rewritten winner with its pre-merge backup).
Refuses to overwrite an existing file at the restore destination (operator must clear first). Refuses if the trash backup is missing.
Does not itself produce a journal entry — redo is 're-run apply'. Keeps the audit trail in one place.

subcommands.name: apply
subcommands.summary: Execute the checked items in plan.md. The only phase that mutates the vault.

flags.description: Actually mutate the vault. Default is dry-run.
Secret response mode override: block | warn | silent. Defaults to the policy's secret_response (default: block).

notes: Default mode is dry-run. --execute is required to actually mutate.
Every action is journaled to journal.jsonl (append-only, fsynced per write) as one of: applied, skipped, refused, dry-run.
Before every mutation, the source file is re-hashed. If the hash has drifted since the plan was written, the action is skipped with reason hash_drift.
Files whose basename has any incoming wikilink or embed are refused (reason linked_note) until --rewrite-links lands.

SECRETS-bucket handling depends on the response mode: block refuses with reason secrets_bucket (default); warn proceeds with a logged warning; silent proceeds quietly. The journal always stamps secret_pattern for audit, regardless of mode.

Secret response modes only matter when the policy's action for SECRETS is mutating (drop, merge-frontmatter). The default policy maps SECRETS to quarantine (no-op), so response modes don't apply unless you override the policy.

merge-frontmatter: for a FRONTMATTER-ONLY pair, paths[1] is the winner (rewritten with union-merged frontmatter) and paths[0] is the loser (moved to trash). Union rule: unique keys kept from both sides; list-valued keys (tags/aliases) set-unioned preserving winner's order; scalar duplicates kept; scalar or type conflicts refuse with reason frontmatter_conflict. The loser moves to trash first so a failed rewrite leaves the winner untouched. The winner is rewritten atomically via temp + rename.
Deletions are soft: files move to .obconverge/trash/<timestamp>/<original-path>. Hard delete is not yet supported.

persistent_flags.description: Override config file (replaces user config; does not merge).
debug | info | warn | error. Overrides config.

buckets.name: TAG-DELTA
buckets.condition: body identical; frontmatter differs only in the top-level tags key

buckets.name: FRONTMATTER-ONLY
buckets.condition: body identical, frontmatter differs (in more than just tags)

buckets.name: APPEND-ONLY
buckets.condition: one side is a byte-prefix of the other

buckets.name: SECRETS
buckets.condition: contains a credential-shaped string; wins over any other bucket

actions.name: merge-frontmatter
actions.description: Union-merge frontmatter keys; drop the losing copy (pending apply).

actions.name: review
actions.description: Flag for human review; apply never touches.

actions.name: quarantine
actions.description: SECRETS-only: apply opens both files in Obsidian and does not modify.

actions.name: keep
actions.description: No-op; file is recognized and left alone.

exit_codes.meaning: usage error (missing flag, bad input, or any other unclassified error)
validation error (bad config, bad policy, bad vault path)
plan required (apply invoked without an approved plan)
hash drift since plan was written (journaled; apply exits 0 today, code 4 reserved for a future --strict mode)
refused by a safety invariant (linked note, SECRETS, protected path) — journaled; apply exits 0 today, code 5 reserved for a future --strict mode

artifacts.name: index.jsonl
artifacts.description: One Entry per regular file in the vault.

artifacts.name: classification.jsonl
artifacts.description: One Record per pair or unique file; includes referrer_count (incoming wikilinks/embeds to the file's basename, resolved via frontmatter aliases).

artifacts.name: plan.md
artifacts.description: Human-reviewable markdown checklist; operator ticks checkboxes to approve.
artifacts.first_lines: Obsidian YAML frontmatter with tool/schema/created fields.

artifacts.name: journal.jsonl
artifacts.description: Append-only log of apply outcomes: one Entry per attempted action with action_id, op, result (applied|skipped|refused|dry-run), path, trash_path, content_hash, expected_hash, actual_hash, reason, referrer_count, applied_at. Previous journals are preserved with a .<timestamp>.bak suffix.

precedence: 3. --config/-c override file (replaces user layer; does not merge)

secret_patterns_source.extension_semantics: Additive only. User patterns are appended after built-ins. A user pattern whose name collides with a built-in (or another user pattern) is a hard error; user extensions can never remove or shadow shipped detectors.
